title: US Treasury exposes nine billion dollar Iranian sanctions evasion through front companies in four countries
summary: The US Treasury sent letters to China, Hong Kong, UAE and Oman revealing Iran processed $9 billion in transactions during 2024 through front companies via banks in those jurisdictions. Treasury warned of enforcement actions against entities involved.
contents:
## US Treasury Exposes $9B Iran Sanctions Evasion Network

### Investigation Findings
The US Treasury Department sent letters on May 3, 2026 to China, Hong Kong, the UAE and Oman, revealing that the Iranian government processed $9 billion in transactions during 2024 involving front companies through banks in those jurisdictions.

### Evasion Methods
Treasury identified the regime's exploitation of the international financial system through:
- Front companies operating across multiple jurisdictions
- Shadow banking networks
- Deceptive practices designed to obscure transaction origins
- Permissive conduits in financial institutions

### Enforcement Warning
Treasury warned that it possesses tools including:
- Enforcement actions for sanctions violations involving US persons
- Secondary sanctions against foreign financial institutions
- Enhanced due diligence requirements on US-processed transactions

### Waiver Expiration
The Treasury Department separately announced that the short-term authorization permitting the sale of Iranian oil already stranded at sea would expire on Sunday and would not receive renewal.

### Strategic Context
The letter urged recipient countries to "identify and stop any illicit activity linked to Iran to avoid further action from Treasury," signaling intensified financial pressure alongside the military blockade.

### Broader Pattern
The findings complement the US military blockade of Iranian ports around the Strait of Hormuz, creating a dual military-financial pressure campaign.
